data.title: Higher Furniture Tariffs Seen as a Boost to La-Z-Boy
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller

President Trump's promise to impose new tariffs on imported furniture sparked concern among investors of most furniture companies--but not La-Z-Boy.

Trump said late Thursday he would slap a 30% tariff on upholstered furniture, but La-Z-Boy shares rose as high as 4.7% in early trading Friday before paring back gains.

The company produces most of its upholstered furniture in the U.S., while other furniture companies rely on imports from Vietnam, China, Malaysia and Indonesia. That could help La-Z-Boy increase sales if its competitors are forced to raise prices to offset tariff costs.

"The vast majority of our product is manufactured here in North America, so we're not as impacted by tariffs. That plays well for us," La-Z-Boy Chief Executive Melinda Whittington said during the company's earnings call last month.

President Trump said in August that the government was investigating furniture imported into the U.S. and would assess levies "at a rate yet to be determined." The White House later clarified that furniture was already part of a continued investigation into imports of timber, lumber and their derivative products, which includes furniture and cabinetry.

Analysts have said the furniture industry could struggle to cope with tariffs, more so than other industries. Furniture manufacturing capacity in the U.S. is limited, meaning companies will have challenges moving production on-shore. Most furniture purchases also are discretionary and as a result, companies could suffer from consumers delaying their purchases if prices go up.

But La-Z-Boy said last month that 90% of its upholstered units sold in North America are produced in the U.S., with its Mexico operations compliant with the U.S.-Mexico-Canada trade agreement supplying most of the rest. "We have been able to navigate the current trade and tariff situation through strategic inventory moves, sourcing adjustments, vendor diversification and nominal pricing actions," Chief Financial Officer Taylor Luebke said last month.

Other furniture companies like Williams-Sonoma and RH fell in early trading Friday.

Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com.

data.description: TikTok-parent ByteDance will likely get about half of the profit from the platform's U.S. operations even after it sells majority ownership to American investors as part of a deal orchestrated by President Donald Trump, Bloomberg News reported on Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.

data.title: STEELCASE INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Steelcase Inc., a global leader in office furniture and workspace solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter. The report highlights the company's financial performance, operational challenges, and strategic initiatives aimed at future growth. The company has experienced mixed results, with revenue growth offset by increased costs and restructuring efforts.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $897.1 million, representing a 5% increase compared to the prior year, driven by growth from large corporate customers and pricing benefits.
  • Gross Profit: $308.5 million, reflecting a gross margin of 34.4%, slightly down from 34.5% in the prior year.
  • Operating Income: $53.1 million, a decrease from $90.0 million in the prior year, impacted by restructuring costs and merger transaction costs.
  • Net Income: $35.0 million, down from $63.1 million in the prior year, with a diluted EPS of $0.29 compared to $0.53 in the prior year.
  • Diluted EPS: $0.29, reflecting a decrease from $0.53 in the prior year, influenced by lower operating income and higher restructuring costs.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The Americas segment, which includes the U.S., Canada, the Caribbean Islands, and Latin America, saw a 3% increase in revenue driven by growth from large corporate customers, partially offset by a decline from education customers. The International segment, covering EMEA and Asia Pacific, experienced a 13% revenue increase, with significant growth in India, China, and the United Kingdom, despite declines in France and Germany.
  • Geographical Performance: In the Americas, revenue growth was primarily driven by large corporate customers, with pricing actions implemented to offset tariff and inflation costs. In the International segment, revenue growth was supported by favorable currency translation effects and strong performance in India, China, and the UK, although France and Germany saw declines.
  • Operational Challenges: The company faced operational challenges in the form of restructuring costs, particularly in the EMEA and Asia Pacific regions, as part of efforts to optimize cost structures and align with market demands.
  • Future Outlook: The company is focused on completing its restructuring actions in EMEA and Asia Pacific by 2027, which are expected to result in cost savings. Additionally, the ongoing investment in a new enterprise resource planning (ERP) system is anticipated to enhance operational efficiencies once implemented.
  • Merger Agreement: Steelcase entered into a merger agreement with HNI Corporation, which is expected to close by the end of 2025, subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals. This merger is anticipated to impact future operational strategies and market positioning.

SEC Filing: STEELCASE INC [ SCS ] - 10-Q - Sep. 26, 2025

data.title: Meta to Launch Ad-Free Service on Facebook, Instagram in U.K. — 2nd Update
data.description:
By Joshua Kirby and Edith Hancock

British users of popular social-media sites Facebook and Instagram will have the option to pay for an advertisement-free service, the sites' owner Meta Platforms said, extending an alternative income stream from the ad payments that drive Meta's revenue.

"Over the coming weeks, we will give people in the U.K. the choice to subscribe to stop seeing ads on Facebook and Instagram," the tech company said Friday. Most of the world's billions of users of Facebook and Instagram currently see advertisements, constituting Meta's main revenue plank at more than $150 billion annually.

U.K. users will be offered the option to pay 3.99 pounds ($5.32) monthly on iOS and Android mobile devices, or 2.99 pounds for web, to use their main accounts without seeing ads, Meta said. Accounts will continue to be personalized, but data won't be used to show ads to users, the company said.

"Those who choose to use our services for free will continue to see ads on our platforms and will still be able to use all of the tools and settings that empower people to control their ads experience," the company said.

The move comes after Meta settled a U.K. legal challenge over its practice of using account-holders' data to serve them personalized advertisements. The Information Commissioner's Office, which supported the challenge, said it was pleased with Meta's plans to offer a subscription model. "This moves Meta away from targeting users with ads as part of the standard terms and conditions for using its Facebook and Instagram services, which we've been clear is not in line with U.K. law," a spokesperson said.

Meta said earlier this year it would consider offering users the option to subscribe to a less personalized service, which it started offering in the European Union in 2023.

The company slashed the cost of that model last November in its attempt to comply with the EU's Digital Markets Act, which obliges large and powerful technology companies like Meta to give users more control over how their data is processed for personal advertising.

The European Commission fined Meta 200 million euros ($233.3 million)in April, saying the system fell foul of the DMA until November 2024, when it slashed the cost of the model. It has been scrutinizing the changes since then, and also gave Meta a June 2025 deadline to come up with a better offer. The company now risks facing further incremental fines from Brussels if officials decide its latest model does not comply with the law.

"EU regulators continue to overreach by requiring us to provide a less personalized ads experience that goes beyond what the law requires, creating a worse experience for users and businesses," Meta said Friday.

"The U.K.'s more pro-growth and pro-innovation regulatory environment will mean more choice for users, continued free access to Facebook and Instagram for those who prefer that option, and ongoing support for businesses who rely on our personalized advertising tools as an engine of growth and productivity," Meta said.

"This approach and outcome sets the U.K. apart from the EU," the company said.

Write to Joshua Kirby at joshua.kirby@wsj.com and Edith Hancock at edith.hancock@wsj.com

data.title: American Trust Investment Services Strengthens Executive Leadership Amid Accelerated Investment Banking Growth and Global Expansion
data.description:
NEW YORK, Sept. 26, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — American Trust Investment Services, Inc. ("ATIS"), a FINRA and SEC-registered boutique investment bank and broker-dealer, today announced key updates to its executive leadership team as the firm continues its strategic transformation and rapid growth across equity capital markets, mergers and acquisitions, and corporate finance advisory.

With an active IPO and ECM pipeline, NASDAQ Capital Markets membership since 2024, and an expanding international footprint, ATIS is positioning itself as a rising force in the boutique investment banking landscape. The firm maintains a strong national presence across the U.S., operates a dedicated team in Hong Kong, and is actively expanding into the Middle East, including Dubai and Abu Dhabi, to better support its growing roster of domestic and international issuers.

"ATIS is entering one of the most dynamic growth phases in our history," said James F. Dever, Chief Executive Officer of ATIS. "We've built a leadership team with the experience and vision to compete at the highest levels of the boutique investment banking space. With our expanding global presence and enhanced market capabilities, we're uniquely positioned to help clients achieve their goals."

Recent ATIS Highlights

  • IPO & ECM Leadership -- ATIS has recently acted as lead underwriter on multiple successful offerings, including Marwynn Holdings (NASDAQ: MWYN), Energys Group (NASDAQ: ENGS), and CIIT, with several additional IPOs and follow-ons slated for the coming quarters.
  • NASDAQ Capital Markets Membership -- In 2024, ATIS became a NASDAQ Capital Markets member, strengthening its execution capabilities and enabling direct market access for issuers and institutional investors.
  • Global Expansion -- With a dedicated team in Hong Kong and planned operations in the UAE, including Dubai and Abu Dhabi, ATIS is extending its reach to meet growing international demand for IPO and advisory expertise.

Executive Leadership Team

James F. Dever — Chief Executive Officer

As CEO, James F. Dever leads ATIS' strategic vision and long-term growth initiatives. With over three decades of experience in capital markets and investment banking, James has positioned ATIS as a trusted partner for issuers and investors navigating complex capital markets.

"Our focus is on scaling intelligently while maintaining the boutique-level client service that defines ATIS," Dever said.

Kristopher "Kit" Kessler — President

Kristopher "Kit" Kessler drives ATIS' client engagement strategy, leveraging more than 20 years of experience in wealth management and corporate finance to deepen relationships with issuers and institutional partners.

"Our ability to align our capabilities with clients' strategic objectives has been key to our success," Kessler said. "This leadership team enables us to scale effectively while continuing to deliver tailored, high-value solutions."

Ian E. Lippy — Chief Operating Officer

Ian E. Lippy oversees ATIS' operations, regulatory strategy, and execution infrastructure. He has been instrumental in scaling ATIS' investment banking platform, implementing systems that support a robust IPO pipeline and expanding cross-border transaction capabilities.

"Our growth is driven by investing in people, technology, and strategic relationships," Lippy said. "With NASDAQ membership, a growing international presence, and a strengthened leadership team, ATIS is positioned to deliver exceptional results for our clients worldwide."

James L. Clarren — Managing Director, Equity Capital Markets

James L. Clarren serves as Managing Director of ATIS' Equity Capital Markets group, overseeing origination and execution for IPOs, follow-ons, and other ECM transactions.

"Our ECM platform is built on precision, agility, and experience," Clarren said. "We provide issuers with seamless execution and strategic insights throughout the entire transaction lifecycle."

Charles J. Slavinsky — Chief Compliance Officer

Charles J. Slavinsky heads ATIS' compliance and risk management programs, leveraging more than 30 years of experience across major wirehouses and boutique firms.

"Integrity, transparency, and robust oversight remain at the core of ATIS' culture," Slavinsky said. "These priorities guide us as we scale our investment banking platform."

About American Trust Investment Services, Inc.

American Trust Investment Services, Inc. ("ATIS") is a FINRA and SEC-registered broker-dealer and boutique investment bank specializing in IPOs, follow-on offerings, and strategic advisory services. Headquartered in the Midwest with offices nationwide, a growing presence in New York, a dedicated team in Hong Kong, and expanding operations in the Middle East, ATIS partners with emerging growth companies to deliver innovative capital markets solutions and long-term value creation.

Media Contact:

Ariella Sherman

CEO, AS Strategic

IR@amtruinvest.com | www.amtruinvest.com

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/american-trust-investment-services-strengthens-executive-leadership-amid-accelerated-investment-banking-growth-and-global-expansion-302568294.html

SOURCE American Trust Investment Services, Inc.

data.title: Ibovespa Rises as Airlines Surge After Merger Break
data.description:
The Ibovespa inched higher to above 145,500 on Friday, as markets assessed the latest corporate news and continued to gauge the outlook on global rates.

Airline stocks surged after the cancellation of the Gol-Azul merger.

Gol was up over 7% and Azul more than 15%.

Brazil’s antitrust regulator had recently pressed the carriers to formalize their codeshare deal and warned against premature merger announcements.

Both confirmed the termination of the codeshare agreement as well.

Petrobras (+1%), major banks (Itaú +0.3%, Bradesco +0.4%), and retailers (Magazine Luiza +2%, GPA +4.5%) advanced.

On the other hand, Braskem lost over 7% after UBS downgraded the stock to “neutral,” after weak results and a sector downturn.

The company hired advisors to explore capital structure alternatives.

Vale (-1.4%) slipped with iron ore.

data.title: Boeing's stock takes off as Max 737 restrictions look set to ease
data.description:
By Claudia Assis

FAA to allow Boeing to make safety checks on 737 Max jets

The Max jet family was grounded for nearly two years starting in March 2019 following two deadly crashes less than five months apart.

Shares of Boeing Co. on Friday were the best performer on the S&P 500 index as investors cheered the prospect of less government oversight over some of the jet maker's production.

The Wall Street Journal reported earlier Friday that the Federal Aviation Administration is set to allow Boeing itself to perform final safety checks on the 737 Max jets, citing people familiar with the matter. That approval could come as early as Friday, the Journal said, citing one of those people.

Boeing's stock (BA) is on track for its highest close in two weeks, and leads Dow Jones Industrial Average DJIA gainers. Boeing is a Dow component.

That would be a welcome sign that Boeing's turnaround may be taking hold after a slew of problems for the jet maker, one of the first being the grounding of the 737 Max fleet for nearly two years starting in March 2019, leading to tighter FAA oversight.

That worldwide grounding followed two deadly crashes less than five months apart involving the jet family.

Boeing's authority to perform the safety sign offs would come in phases, and the FAA would remain involved in issuing airworthiness certificates, a document required for each plane to fly with passengers, the Journal said.

Boeing shares are up 26% so far this year, compared with an advance of about 12% for the S&P 500 index. SPX

-Claudia Assis

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Siemens USA President, CEO Barbara Humpton to Retire
data.description:
By Colin Kellaher

Siemens said Barbara Humpton, president and chief executive of its Siemens USA arm, is retiring from the global technology company on Oct. 1.

Siemens on Friday said Ann Fairchild, general counsel for Siemens USA, will step in as interim president and CEO of the unit, which employs 45,000 people across the U.S.

Humpton joined Siemens in 2011 and has spent the last seven years at the helm of Siemens USA, which serves the German company's largest market and generated $21.1 billion in revenue in fiscal 2024.

Write to Colin Kellaher at colin.kellaher@wsj.com

data.title: The Payments Group Holding GmbH & Co. KGaA: The Payments Group Holding receives final approval from the MFSA for the acquisition of The Payments Group
data.description:
The Payments Group Holding GmbH & Co. KGaA / Key word(s): Mergers & Acquisitions

The Payments Group Holding GmbH & Co. KGaA: The Payments Group Holding receives final approval from the MFSA for the acquisition of The Payments Group

26-Sep-2025 / 16:37 CET/CEST

Disclosure of an inside information acc. to Article 17 MAR of the Regulation (EU) No 596/2014, transmitted by EQS News - a service of EQS Group.

The issuer is solely responsible for the content of this announcement.

+++ Ad-hoc Announcement +++

The Payments Group Holding receives final approval from the MFSA

for the acquisition of The Payments Group

  • Closing expected in the upcoming months
  • Negotiations with investors to fund the cash tranche have already begun

Frankfurt/Main, 26 September 2025 –The Payments Group Holding (PGH), a holding company based in Frankfurt am Main that was founded in 2012 and renamed in August 2024, has received final written approval from Malta's financial supervisory authority, the Malta Financial Services Authority (MFSA), to acquire the licensed e-money institution Calida Financial Ltd. This clears the way for the acquisition of The Payments Group (TPG), a group of four cooperating, specialized fintech and paytech companies incl. Calida Financial Ltd. operating under this name.

The management expects the closing in the upcoming months with the acquisition of 72.9% respectively 75% stake in the target companies. The closing is thus solely in the hands of PGH for the first time. It requires the funding of the cash tranche to be paid by PGH as part of the compensation for the acquisition through a one-time placement of treasury shares. This is to take place outside the capital market with investors specializing in the online payment sector. PGH has already entered into negotiations with interested, financially strong investors.

The remaining compensation for the acquisition will be made through a transfer of PGH shares. The management expects that PGH will have to transfer 19 to 23 million PGH shares. The number of outstanding PGH shares currently amounts to 11.4 million. In addition, PGH holds 34.9 million treasury shares.

For the remaining 25% of the target companies, PGH holds a call option until 31 January 2027.

About The Payments Group Holding

The Payments Group Holding (PGH) is a holding company and venture capital provider based in Frankfurt am Main founded in 2012 and renamed in August 2024.

In August 2024, PGH signed a share purchase agreement for the acquisition of four PayTech companies, which is expected to close in summer 2025 subject to certain conditions precedent. After closing of the transaction, PGH will form a group of four operating PayTech companies:

Funanga AG, Campamocha Ltd with its 100%-owned subsidiaries TBWS Ltd and Calida Financial Ltd as well as Surfer Rosa Ltd form a fast-growing, vertically integrated e-Money PayTech group of companies - The Payments Group (TPG). TPG provides proprietary closed and open (branded and white-labelled) prepaid payment services to hundreds of online merchants worldwide. The synergy between these companies positions TPG as the future market leader in embedded financial products and prepaid solutions. Calida Financial Ltd. is the regulated company within TPG, having received an e-money license from the Malta Financial Services Authority (MFSA) in August 2024. This license entitles Calida Financial Ltd. to offer innovative e-money services and products across Europe.

TPG employs over 50 people and operates globally. TPG's customers make use of more than 550,000 POS cash payment points and the global online prepaid card network to process cash and cashless online payments.

Furthermore, PGH operates an AI-focused company builder called 'Softmax AI' together with AI experts via its future 25% stake in German AI Projects GmbH. In addition, The Payments Group Holding holds from its history as a leading German venture capital provider under the German Startups Group brand a heritage VC-portfolio of minority stakes in partly promising German startups via its wholly owned subsidiary German Startups Group VC GmbH.

More information: www.tpgholding.com.

Investor Relations ContactRosenberg Strategic Communications

Alexander Schmidt

a.schmidt@rosenbergsc.com

data.title: CRACKER BARREL OLD COUNTRY STORE, INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Cracker Barrel Old Country Store, Inc., a renowned American chain of combined restaurant and gift stores with a Southern country theme, has released its annual Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

While the specific financial metrics such as Revenue, Gross Profit, Operating Income, Net Income, and Net Income Per Share were not detailed in the provided text, these metrics are typically found in Part 2 Item 8 and Part 2 Item 7 of the Form 10-K report. For a complete understanding of Cracker Barrel's financial performance, stakeholders are encouraged to review these sections in the full report.

Business Highlights

  • Cracker Barrel Store Operations: As of September 12, 2025, Cracker Barrel operated 657 stores across 43 states, with no stores franchised. The stores feature a rustic old country-store design with a full-service restaurant and a gift shop.
  • Maple Street Biscuit Company Operations: The company operated 68 Maple Street Biscuit Company locations in 10 states, focusing on breakfast and lunch with a fast-casual concept.
  • Revenue Segments: In 2025, approximately 81% of total revenue was generated from restaurant operations, with the remaining from retail sales. The average check per guest was $15.23, a 6.8% increase from the previous year.
  • Product Offerings: Cracker Barrel's menu includes home-style country cooking with breakfast available all day. The gift shops offer items inspired by the restaurant menu and other decorative and functional items.
  • Off-Premise Business: Approximately 20% of restaurant sales were generated through off-premise channels, including Individual To Go, Third-Party Delivery, and Catering and Occasion.
  • Retail Sales Breakdown: The largest shares of retail sales in 2025 were apparel and accessories (33%), food (18%), toys (13%), décor (13%), and media (7%).
  • Purchasing and Distribution: The company sources food products through a contract with an unaffiliated distributor and purchases produce and dairy through a network of suppliers. Retail items are primarily warehoused in Lebanon, Tennessee.
  • Technology Integration: Cracker Barrel leverages technology to enhance guest experiences, manage operations, and support marketing efforts, including a digital waitlist and mobile payments.
  • Employee Development: The company emphasizes employee training and development, offering comprehensive benefits and a focus on operational excellence.
  • Quality Assurance: Cracker Barrel maintains robust quality assurance and food safety processes, including third-party audits and comprehensive training for employees.
  • Marketing Strategy: The company uses multiple media channels, including over 1,300 billboards, digital marketing, and a customer loyalty program to engage guests.
  • Store Development: In 2025, one new Cracker Barrel store was opened, and two were closed. The company plans to open two new stores in 2026.
  • Future Outlook: The company is focused on driving relevancy, delivering a guest experience that is loved, and growing profitability through strategic initiatives and capital allocation.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: The company is focused on enhancing shareholder value through strategic equity management and maintaining a strong market presence. This includes the ongoing evaluation of capital allocation strategies to support growth and operational efficiency.
  • Capital Management: The company’s common stock is traded on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the symbol 'CBRL'. As of September 12, 2025, there were 5,832 shareholders of record. The company has implemented dividend policies and share repurchase programs as part of its capital management strategy. Details on dividend restrictions and share-based compensation awards are provided in the financial statements and management discussions.
  • Future Outlook: The company aims to continue its strategic focus on capital management to support long-term growth and shareholder returns. This includes potential adjustments to dividend policies and share repurchase activities based on market conditions and financial performance.

Challenges and Risks

  • Operational Risks: Cracker Barrel faces significant challenges related to its reliance on a single retail distribution facility in Lebanon, Tennessee. This centralization poses risks in the event of natural disasters or public health crises, which could disrupt operations and supply chains. Additionally, the company is heavily dependent on certain foreign-sourced retail products, particularly from the People’s Republic of China, exposing it to risks such as tariffs, trade barriers, and political instability.
  • Macroeconomic and Industry-Specific Risks: The company is exposed to various macroeconomic and industry-specific risks, including purchase price volatility and inflationary pressures affecting food, ingredients, and labor costs. These factors could adversely impact operating margins and financial performance. The competitive labor market in the U.S. has led to upward pressure on wages, which may continue to affect the company's cost structure. Furthermore, the company faces risks from pandemics and public health concerns, which could reduce consumer traffic and disrupt supply chains.
  • Reputational Risks: Cracker Barrel also faces significant operational risks from unfavorable publicity and the impact of social media, which can quickly influence consumer perceptions and behavior. The failure to achieve the goals of its multi-year strategic plan, including initiatives to enhance menu and retail options, could adversely affect financial performance. Intense competition in the restaurant and retail industries poses ongoing challenges, with competitors potentially impacting customer and employee retention through aggressive marketing and hiring practices.
  • Market Risks: The company is subject to market risks related to fluctuations in commodity prices, particularly for beef, poultry, and dairy products, which are significant components of its food purchasing expenses. Cracker Barrel is also exposed to risks from changes in interest rates and foreign currency exchange rates, which could impact its financial results. The company's capital structure, including its indebtedness and related covenants, may limit financial flexibility and increase borrowing costs, posing additional risks to liquidity and operational stability.

SEC Filing: CRACKER BARREL OLD COUNTRY STORE, INC [ CBRL ] - 10-K - Sep. 26, 2025

data.title: Bearish bets build: stocks set for a bumpy ride
data.description:
Key points:
  • S&P 500, Dow rise; Nasdaq falls
  • Energy up most among S&P sectors; tech biggest percentage loser
  • Euro STOXX 600 index up ~0.7%
  • Dollar, bitcoin dip; gold, crude rise
  • U.S. 10-Year Treasury yield steady at ~4.17%

BEARISH BETS BUILD: STOCKS SET FOR A BUMPY RIDE

Wall Street's main indexes hit have hit record highs, but short selling has set new monthly records since February as investors bet tariffs would pressure stocks, Brian Reynolds, chief market strategist at Reynolds Strategy LLC, said in a note.

He added that investors built even more short positions in the first half of September, setting up a volatile end to the year.

"With year-end on the horizon, and with so many short positions, there remains the potential for stocks to melt up later this year and in early 2026 should margin clerks force short sellers to cover," he said.

These bearish positions stand in contrast to the record amount of money flowing to the credit market from U.S. public pensions and insurance companies, Reynolds said.

"If short selling continues apace, the VIX curve could invert as it did in March, stoking a brief stock market correction," Reynolds said.

For now, stocks have been fluctuating around 1 standard deviation above trend in recent weeks, Reynolds noted. "Should they approach 2 standard deviations above trend, we would continue to advocate selling the most overheated names in a portfolio," he added.

The CBOE volatility index , investors' fear gauge, spiked to a more than three-week high on Thursday as stronger than expected data dampened hopes of two more interest rates cuts this year.

(Medha Singh)

****

data.title: Gold miners gain as bullion prices edge up on Fed rate cut hopes
data.description:
** Shares of gold miners up, tracking rise in bullion prices

** Spot gold up 0.5% at $3,661.56/ounce, after hitting a record $3,790.82/ounce earlier in the week

** The metal has risen 2% this week

data.title: With US backing, Argentina gains time - but October vote will be crucial
data.description:
Key points:
  • Milei party's provincial vote underperformance roiled markets
  • Central bank burnt through more than $1 bln to shore up peso
  • U.S. intervention spurred massive rally in stocks, bonds, peso
  • All eyes on October 26 election, FX measures afterwards

By Libby George

Argentina's "whatever it takes" moment with the United States this week may have given the country its best chance to escape decades of chaotic debt defaults and bailouts, investors say. But everything hinges on October's nationwide election.

Argentine assets soared after U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said "all options" were on the table to stabilize the country and support President Javier Milei, an ideological ally whose chainsaw-style austerity drive has been politically costly - as his September 7 provincial Buenos Aires election drubbing showed.

Washington's intervention partially reversed the market nosedive triggered by the underperformance of Milei's party in the provincial vote, a stumble that fanned fears of a broader loss in the October 26 nationwide ballot that could slow or block his reform agenda.

"This is an unprecedented support from the United States," said Gustavo Medeiros, head of research at Ashmore Group.

Milei's radical reforms since becoming president in December 2023 had helped address the solvency issue Argentina had, Medeiros said, but concerns over a cashflow crunch persisted.

"(Argentina) had a liquidity problem until the announcement... Now it doesn't have either," he said.

But whether the U.S. pledges augur a lasting change in direction hinges on how Milei's party and its allies perform in October.

"It really comes back to the outcome of the elections and whether you have the domestic support to continue the reforms they need," said Joyce Chang, global head of research at JPMorgan.

SWAPLINES AND BOND SUPPORT

Bessent on Wednesday outlined a package that included a $20 billion swap line, U.S. purchases of Argentina's dollar-denominated bonds in primary and secondary markets, and significant standby credit from the Exchange Stabilization Fund.

The infusion of U.S. support is the latest - albeit brightest - glimmer of hope for the Latin American behemoth to change its reputation as a byword for sovereign financial mismanagement.

While terms and conditions remain unclear, the might of the U.S. signal alone is helping bridge a tricky period, investors say.

"It's more about being forceful with the rhetoric so that the number matters less," said Carmen Altenkirch, emerging markets sovereign analyst with Aviva Investors.

Argentina has burned investors with false breakthroughs before. In 2017, investors - lured by faith in then-president Mauricio Macri to implement reforms - snapped up a $2.75 billion century bond. Two years later, he lost power and by 2020 the country had defaulted again.

That history has left households and companies with the "muscle memory" to hoard dollars at the first hint of turbulence, Medeiros said, a reflex that can drain reserves and amplify stress.

Frayed nerves resurfaced after the provincial Buenos Aires elections. Markets slid, and last week the central bank burned through roughly $1.1 billion of its scarce reserves in just three days to shore up the peso.

KEY TEST IN OCTOBER

Supporters of Milei's reform push argue that 2025 looks different.

They point to fiscal consolidation, cooling inflation and a 2024 fiscal surplus, the first in decades, which together have lured investors back. The promised swap line and potential U.S. dollar inflows are expected to steady the currency - potentially boosting the electoral prospects for Milei's coalition.

A suspension of export taxes on key grains hit its goal of boosting dollar inflows ahead of schedule, allowing the government to reimpose the taxes after just two days.

If Milei's La Libertad Avanza (LLA) and center-right allies the Propuesta Republicana (PRO) secure at least the expected one-third of seats in the Lower House, the government will likely keep the centre-left opposition at bay and retain leverage to keep reforms moving.

"It's hard not to be constructive here," said Thierry Larose, a portfolio manager with Vontobel.

"This announcement is a victory that can restore some confidence in the government and help LLA+PRO maintain their lead in the polls ahead of next month's midterms."

Citi, keeping its overweight on Argentina through the rally, said "governability risks" would persist, but argued that Milei could continue to push through reforms, with his coalition set to retain the numbers it needs to sustain a presidential veto even under the least favourable outcome.

IMF KEEPING UP REFORM PRESSURE

Argentina's other key backer, the International Monetary Fund, will also keep up pressure for painful reforms. Argentina's total credit outstanding to the Fund stood at just under $60 billion by late September - nearly four times the amount of its next-biggest debtor, Ukraine.

The IMF has a laundry list for Milei including rebuilding foreign exchange reserves, moving towards a more flexible exchange rate, overhauling labour rules and advancing privatizations.

The reforms could be painful for a population of 46 million scarred by repeated inflation spikes and currency crises. But any slippage risks triggering yet another market backlash.

"It's worth noting that this bailout package is probably the government's last 'Get out of jail free' card," Larose said. "Investors haven't forgotten the fiscal mismanagement of the past."

data.title: Southwest Airlines to pay $18.5 million over employees' military leave
data.description:
By Jonathan Stempel

Southwest Airlines agreed to pay $18.5 million to settle a class action accusing the carrier of failing to provide paid leave to employees who took short-term military leave.

A preliminary settlement was filed on Thursday night in San Francisco federal court, and requires a judge's approval.

Employees said Southwest wrongly denied pay when they took 14 days or fewer of military leave, despite paying for other absences including sick leave, bereavement leave and jury duty.

They said Southwest's actions violated the Uniformed Services Employment and Reemployment Rights Act, a federal law protecting military personnel in their civilian employment.

Approximately 2,791 eligible Southwest employees would receive an average $4,421, after legal fees of up to one-third of the settlement fund are deducted, court papers show.

Southwest would also provide up to 10 days of paid short-term military leave per calendar year from 2026 to 2030.

The Dallas-based carrier denied wrongdoing and believes military leave is not comparable to other forms of leave, but settled to avoid the risk and cost of litigation, court papers show.

Southwest had no immediate comment on Friday.

In 2019, another San Francisco judge approved a settlement worth up to $18.8 million for nearly 2,000 Southwest pilots also denied paid leave for short-term military service.

The case is Huntsman v Southwest Airlines Co, U.S. District Court, Northern District of California, No. 19-00083.

data.title: Boeing Stock Pops as Its 'Best Sales Person' Lands Another Order — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

President Donald Trump is becoming one of Boeing's best sales advocates.

Shares of the commercial jet maker are up 4.2% at $222.54 early Friday after Turkish Airlines agreed to purchase 75 Boeing 787 twin-aisle jets and up to 150 smaller 737 MAX single-aisle jets. The order and intent to purchase came after Turkish President Tayyip Erdogan met with Trump at the White House on Thursday.

Separately on Thursday, Boeing announced a new order for 30 737 MAX jets from Norwegian Group.

Boeing is a large U.S. exporter, and the president has helped win new orders from the U.K., Korea, and Japan as part of his trade negotiations.

Coming into Friday trading, Boeing stock is up 21% year to date. However, shares are down about 9% in September, which is historically a weak month for shares.

Investors might be concerned with delays for Boeing's huge 777X jet. The plane hasn't been certified by aviation regulators, and initial deliveries could slip from late 2026 into 2027. Delays could result in financial charges, with airlines looking for compensation.

Through August, Boeing had amassed firm orders for 725 jets, up from 250 over the same span in 2024. Boeing has unfilled orders for more than 6,500 jets.

Now, Boeing needs to build the jets. Boeing delivered fewer than 350 planes in 2024 as quality problems constrained 737 MAX production. Wall Street projects about 580 deliveries for 2025 and 675 deliveries for 2026.

Boeing delivered more than 800 planes in 2018. Analysts see Boeing surpassing that number in 2028.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Arabica coffee extends rebound on falling stocks
data.description:
Arabica coffee futures on ICE extended this week's rebound on Friday, boosted by declining exchange stocks, while cocoa prices slumped to two-month lows.

COFFEE

* Arabica coffee rose 1.4% to $3.5625 per lb by 1403 GMT and was on track for a weekly gain of 2.9%. The price had fallen by 12% the previous week.

* The market has regained some ground after a recent decline sparked by an improving outlook for next year's crop in Brazil following rains.

* "Prices have rebounded, spurred by expectations of tighter US ICE-certified supplies following the implementation of a 50% tariff by the U.S. on Brazilian imports," analyst BMI said in a note on Friday.

* Exchange-certified stocks stood at 579,961 bags as of September 25, down 21,756 bags from a day earlier. A month earlier they stood at 729,934 bags.

* The market is also awaiting a planned meeting next week between U.S. President Donald Trump and Brazilian President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva to see if a 50% U.S. tariff on Brazilian coffee could be revised.

* Robusta coffee rose 1.9% to $4,158 a metric ton.

COCOA

* London cocoa lost 0.3% to 4,873 pounds a ton after hitting a two-month low of 4,851 pounds.

* Dealers said concerns about weak demand continued to weigh on prices and third-quarter grind data for Europe, North America and Asia in early October would be closely watched. The European grind data is expected to be issued on October 16.

* New York cocoa was down 0.1% at $6,949 a ton after setting a two-month low of $6,880.

SUGAR

* Raw sugar rose 0.6% to 15.86 cents per lb and was on track for a weekly gain of 2.6%.

* Sugar production in Brazil's key centre-south region in the first half of September is likely to have risen 15% year on year to 3.6 million tons, according to a survey of analysts published by S&P Global Commodity Insights on Thursday.

* White sugar was down 0.6% at $460.10 a ton.

data.title: Kremlin says talk of shooting down Russian planes is a serious escalation of tensions
data.description:
The Kremlin said on Friday that talk of shooting down Russian military planes over Europe was reckless, aggressive and marked a serious escalation of tension near Russia's border.

"Statements about shooting down Russian planes are, well, at the very least, reckless, irresponsible. And, of course, dangerous in their consequences," Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov told state TV reporter Pavel Zarubin.

Bloomberg reported earlier that European diplomats had warned Moscow that NATO was ready to respond with full force against violations of its airspace, including by shooting down Russian planes.

Peskov repeated Moscow's denial that its fighter planes had intruded into Estonian air space last week.

"We heard the exchanges when our planes were accused of violating Estonian airspace. However, absolutely no evidence, let alone convincing evidence, was presented," he said.

"And, of course, against the backdrop of such recklessness, making such aggressive statements, this is another very significant round of escalation of tensions near our border."
